prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident has been detected involving the service account `svc_etl` on host `10.20.3.15`. Approximately 1.1GB of sensitive data, identified as FINANCE_PII, was uploaded to a suspicious external domain, `upload.evil-cdn.net`, via a `curl` process. Multiple security systems flagged this activity as a data exfiltration pattern and a MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel.

timeline.event: Service account `svc_etl` initiates a connection to `upload.evil-cdn.net:443` from `10.20.3.15`.
Firewall detects an outbound anomaly: `10.20.3.15` sent 1.1GB to `185.220.101.44` in 77 seconds.
DLP identifies sensitive label `FINANCE_PII` in outbound transfer by `svc_etl`.
SIEM detects a data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to rare external domain.
IDS suspects MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host `10.20.3.15`.
Proxy logs TLS SNI for `upload.evil-cdn.net` used by client `svc_etl`.
Netflow reports a 14x volume spike from `10.20.3.15` to `185.220.101.44:443`.
SIEM issues a CRITICAL alert for data exfiltration by user `svc_etl` to `upload.evil-cdn.net`.
EDR shows process `curl` initiated by `svc_etl` uploading `/data/finance_q2.zip`.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host `10.20.3.15` from the network to prevent further data loss or lateral movement.
Disable or reset credentials for the `svc_etl` account and investigate its activity for signs of compromise.
Block all traffic to `upload.evil-cdn.net` and IP address `185.220.101.44` at the perimeter firewall and proxy.
Conduct a full forensic analysis on host `10.20.3.15` to determine the initial compromise vector, persistence mechanisms, and full scope of exfiltrated data.
Review access logs for `/data/finance_q2.zip` and other sensitive data stores to identify any unauthorized access prior to exfiltration.
Notify relevant stakeholders (e.g., legal, privacy, management) about the data breach involving `FINANCE_PII`.
